bullets.text: Progressive reported a net loss of $70 million for 2008, its first in 26 years, after recognizing net realized losses including other-than-temporary impairments of ~$1.4 billion, or about 10% of invested assets.

Full-year combined ratio was 94.6, well within the 96 target; pretax underwriting income was ~$735 million with 42 of 50 established states profitable, including New York auto which had been a concern in 2007.

Capital loss was contained to the excess layer held at the holding company; at year-end Progressive remained several hundred million above the sum of regulatory and extreme contingency capital layers.

1-, 3-, and 5-year written premium growth was -1%, -1%, and 3% respectively; Progressive ended 2008 up 3% in policies in force, adding 350,000 policies, with Direct auto growing 9% in policies and 4% in written premium.
Direct auto grew 9% in policies and 4% in written premium; Agency auto was the biggest challenge with lost momentum in the nonstandard niche; Commercial Auto ended essentially flat on policies.
Direct channel now ~38% of policies versus Agency at ~62%; Direct growth is characterized as a referendum on changing consumer shopping behavior with Internet production leading all channels.
Claims quality reached best-ever levels for the year; concierge centers provided insights driving process redesign; reduced claims frequency created challenges in maintaining staffing balance.

The 'Flo' Superstore campaign debuted in 2008, exceeding prior advertising efforts by all measurement analytics; the Progressive Automotive X PRIZE was also announced as a $10 million brand sponsorship.

Name Your Price launched, Progressive Home Advantage expanded to 46 states for Direct and 33 for Agency, and MyRate usage-based insurance expanded across states, all aimed at broadening consumer reach.

IT organization undertook a comprehensive reassessment of all processes and priorities; a multi-year policy processing system replacement went live in its first state during 2009 with excellent quality results.
